title: strychnos sellowiana
intro: Strychnos sellowiana, also known as the Sellow's nux vomica, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. The plant contains the toxic alkaloid brucine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The brucine in Strychnos sellowiana has been reported to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as pain relief and reduced inflammation. In traditional herbal medicine, Strychnos sellowiana is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to alleviate various health conditions.
